channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: The memory shortage shaking Apple and Microsoft is 'existential crisis' for smaller players
item.description: While Apple and Microsoft raise prices on key devices to help cover the soaring costs of memory, smaller consumer electronics companies are in dire straits.

item.title: Tanker struck in Strait of Hormuz as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate
item.description: The attacks come as the United States and Iran are supposed to be engaging in a 60-day ceasefire as they attempt to work toward a resolution.

item.title: SpaceX to join the Nasdaq-100 in a fast-tracked process that will drive huge ETF buying demand
item.description: Adding SpaceX this quickly would make the Elon Musk company one of the first beneficiaries of Nasdaq's recently adopted fast-track inclusion framework.

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela earthquakes tops 1,400
item.description: Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on state television that 10 more countries were still to join rescue efforts.

item.title: Berkshire CEO Greg Abel sworn in as U.S. citizen at baseball game
item.description: Abel, a longtime Iowa resident who was born in the Canadian city of Edmonton in 1962, was among the roughly two dozen people from 16 countries who participated in an annual naturalization ceremony hosted by the Iowa Cubs Thursday night in Des Moines.

item.title: Bank of America says these five stocks are best positioned for upside amid market uncertainty
item.description: Analysts at Bank of America said these stocks have plenty of room to run.

item.title: Why investors may want to prioritize bond markets outside the U.S.
item.description: Allspring Global Investments is pushing clients toward countries with central banks that are raising interest rates or have different inflation dynamics.

item.title: SpaceX stock has cooled. Hiring for jobs in the space economy hasn't
item.description: The SpaceX IPO euphoria is over, but the bullish trend in space economy jobs remains in place within a labor market where many other sectors have slowed hiring.

item.title: How GE Vernova builds the massive gas turbines powering the AI data center boom
item.description: GE Vernova turbines are powering Elon Musk's xAI Colossus 1 data center and Microsoft just bought seven to power its data center in Texas.

item.title: Pinterest has taken a beating in recent years. This analyst thinks it's time to buy
item.description: Pinterest has been fallen 24% this year, but analysts are split whether to buy or sell this stock

item.title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ fuels a boom in gay romance stories, with women leading the fandom
item.description: ‘Heated Rivalry’ has helped gay romance stories gain mainstream attention, with women driving much of the fandom.

item.title: From concerts to train rides, bots are winning the ticket wars — but they're only part of the problem
item.description: From concerts to train reservations, automated bots have become the latest target in the fight against ticket scalping.

item.title: How Kohl's lost its way — and is trying to become relevant again
item.description: Kohl's went from a household name to a plunging stock as it lost relevancy and its core customer. Now, the company is trying to turn itself around.

item.title: These stocks are the most oversold after a turbulent week for the market
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange and CME were among the most oversold stocks this week amid ongoing concerns over a regulatory spat concerning perpetual futures.

item.title: Before there were Trump Accounts, SEED OK gave some newborns $1,000 — how researchers say the grants affected kids
item.description: Some state-sponsored programs paved the way for Trump Accounts, the new tax-deferred investing accounts for children.

item.title: American couple left New York City and bought a house in Italy for $13,000: 'We found a different way of life'
item.description: Cassandra Tresl and Alex Ninman moved in with Tresl's grandfather in the Czech Republic in 2020 and closed on a house in Abruzzo, Italy, in 2022.

item.title: Trading in these two ETFs suggests inflation fears are overblown
item.description: It could have been a big week for bond bears, if it weren't for crude oil.

item.title: Red Lobster's Ultimate Endless Shrimp promotion is described as a 'car crash' for the company, lawsuit says
item.description: "Thai Union doubled down on a campaign to squeeze out every drop of value that it could," creditors said

item.title: Boost portfolio income in the second half of 2026 with these cheap dividend payers
item.description: In an expensive stock market, these stocks pay investors to wait for potential upside. They are also well-liked by Wall Street.

item.title: Trump admin allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI model to some companies, government agencies
item.description: Anthropic disabled access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models to comply with a government export control directive citing "national security authorities."

item.title: The AI trade cooled and oil sank. A closer look at Wall Street's volatile week
item.description: Micron finished the week in the red despite a blockbuster earnings report. But falling oil prices was good news for the fight against inflation.

item.title: Alliant Credit Union's new checking account pays up to 2% APY — see how it stacks up
item.description: Alliant Credit Union's new jumbo high-rate checking account pays up to 2% APY with no cap on earnings. Here's what to know.

item.title: Australia toughens kids' social media ban, doubles potential penalties for tech firms
item.description: eSafety is actively investigating ⁠possible non-compliance by five platforms: Meta's Instagram and Facebook, Google's YouTube, Snap's Snapchat, and TikTok.

item.title: OpenAI hasn't held pre-IPO investor meetings or set timeline yet, sources say
item.description: OpenAI said it confidentially filed its prospectus with the SEC earlier this month, but noted it "may be a while" before it goes public.

item.title: OpenAI limits new AI models to 'trusted partners' at request of U.S. government
item.description: OpenAI said it previewed the new models' capabilities with the government ahead of the launch.

item.title: Saudi Aramco resumes oil loading at Ras Tanura in boost to supply
item.description: The Saudi oil loadings come even though a ship belonging to Taiwan's Evergreen Marine was hit by an unknown object in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. crude oil falls below $70, resuming losses after attack on cargo ship near Oman
item.description: Oil extended declines on Friday, with markets focused any further breakthroughs in the Middle East conflict, while absorbing a flurry of Persian Gulf news.

item.title: Red-alert heatwaves are becoming Europe's new normal. Investors are paying attention
item.description: Temperature records were smashed across Europe this week, prompting several countries across the continent to issued high-level warnings about danger to life.

item.title: China industrial profits stay resilient as economy leans on factories, exports
item.description: China's economic growth remains fragile, hobbled by a prolonged property downturn and deep structural imbalances.

item.title: Trump eases pressure on Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh as inflation tops 4%
item.description: President Donald Trump's economic advisors are giving Warsh space, as the new Fed chairman, on interest rates as the president repeats calls to cut.
